reviewer: retrospective critique backfill (code-grounded + existing conformance suite), revision 1

rubric.evidence: The demo runs a REAL in-browser zero-shot-object-detection pass: worker.js imports the shared loadPipeline (lib/webai.js) and calls pipe(imageURL, queries, {threshold:0.01, percentage:false}) on the zero-shot-object-detection pipeline — free-text queries are embedded and matched to image regions, returning [{label, score, box}] in source pixels. No canned result. Grounded in the actual worker source + the immutable conformance suite, which tests `loads-clean` (console-clean, headless Chrome) and `calls-real-inference` (source pattern: pipeline(|createModelLoader|...). Detection classes are whatever text you type — no fixed label set; the very low floor (0.01) means the score slider reveals candidates from a real, unfiltered run. NOTE: this is a retrospective code+suite-grounded score — no fresh measured latency/screenshot was captured in this backfill pass, hence 4 not 5.
rubric.notes: Inference runs off the main thread in a dedicated module Web Worker (new Worker(worker.js, {type:'module'})); Runs once per query set and filters the cached boxes as the slider drags, so the slider never re-runs the model.

rubric.evidence: Expected output = boxes for each free-text query. drawDetections() renders labelled boxes on <canvas id=stage aria-label='Detection result: image with labelled boxes'>; the See-inside table + count chips list each detection with its matched query label and score.
rubric.notes: OWLv2 is the heavy, accurate open-vocabulary detector (~400 MB q8); a single WASM forward pass is noticeably slower than the fixed-class detectors — an honest cost surfaced by the size in at-a-glance.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (Xenova/owlv2-base-patch16-ensemble, linked to its HF card), the real pipeline task (zero-shot-object-detection), backend (WASM — no WebGPU needed), quantisation (q8), download size (~400 MB) and license (Apache-2.0). The worker's loadPipeline call and the createModelLoader model config both use the SAME id/dtype/sizeMB — no invented API surface.

The page loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (model:{modelId:'Xenova/owlv2-base-patch16-ensemble', runtime:'transformers.js', dtype:'q8', sizeMB:400, requiresWebGPU:false}) — no hand-rolled Load button. A valid current on-device model auto-initialises (checking → initialising → ready); only Download/Re-download/Update surface otherwise. Conformance tests `shared-auto-init-loader` (source: createModelLoader).
rubric.notes: modelId keyed on Xenova/owlv2-base-patch16-ensemble so the ~400 MB cache is tracked against the never-silently-re-download guarantee.

rubric.evidence: Loading/ready/inference state is announced in role=status aria-live regions (2 aria-live=polite regions in the page). Failures surface on the page as a readable `.status.err` message ("Detection failed: …"), never only in the console; the shared loader exposes Retry/re-download recovery. Runs on the WASM path everywhere — no WebGPU gate. Conformance tests `live-status-region`, `errors-on-the-page`.

Real keyboard-operable controls: a query text input (comma/newline separated free-text objects), a file dropzone (role=button), sample thumbnails (role=button, Enter/Space), Detect button and a Score-threshold slider with an <output> mirror.

No canned/hardcoded output: every visible result is derived from the real worker run — boxes, matched labels and scores are the raw pipe() output for the typed queries. Conformance carries `no-fake-output` (manual-evidenced) and `calls-real-inference`.

Result canvas carries a descriptive aria-label; sample thumbnails have alt text; the query field is labelled; status uses role=status aria-live; buttons have names. Conformance tests `images-have-alt` and `buttons-have-names` (both script assertions over the DOM).
rubric.notes: models.json records support.desktop/mobile = needs-review with an automated pre-scan flag of several sub-24px tap targets (sample thumbnails / small chips) 'agent to verify tap size' — a genuine open item, not yet manually confirmed.

rubric.evidence: Layout uses the shared design system (public/styles.css) with fluid flex/grid, flex-wrap and intrinsic sizing; conformance carries `responsive-desktop` and `responsive-mobile` (no-horizontal-overflow at ≈1280×800 and ≈360×740 DPR3 touch) plus `parity-both-classes`.
rubric.notes: support.desktop/mobile are still needs-review in models.json (awaiting an agent parity verdict); models.json records support.desktop/mobile = needs-review with an automated pre-scan flag of several sub-24px tap targets (sample thumbnails / small chips) 'agent to verify tap size' — a genuine open item, not yet manually confirmed.

rubric.evidence: Inference runs off the main thread in a dedicated module Web Worker (new Worker(worker.js, {type:'module'})); The threshold slider filters cached boxes with no re-inference. No main-thread inference or unbounded main-thread loop — INP stays low. Conformance carries `inference-off-main-thread` (source) and `inp-stays-low`.
rubric.notes: Inference itself is slow on the WASM path at this model size, but it does not block the UI (off-thread); the interaction cost (slider) stays instant.

guidanceConsulted.query: expose canvas content to assistive technology overlay boxes
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Give canvas-rendered content a meaningful accessible name and a text equivalent so assistive tech, translation and reading mode can reach it.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — labelled detection canvas + text See-inside table for the free-text matches.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low off main thread heavy work in a web worker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy work off the main thread and keep interactions responsive (INP); avoid long blocking tasks on the main thread.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the ~400 MB model's forward pass runs entirely in worker.js so the slow WASM inference never freezes the page; slider filters cached boxes.

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status role status aria-live; every control keyboard-operable with a name
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; give every control an accessible name; keep all controls keyboard-operable.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live, labelled query input, keyboard-operable dropzone/thumbnails, named buttons.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive layout no horizontal overflow fluid grid intrinsic sizing
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use fluid flex/grid with wrap + intrinsic sizing and logical properties; avoid fixed widths that force horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — fluid flex-wrap layout + full-width canvas from the design system.

openQuestions: ~400 MB q8 is a heavy mobile download and WASM inference is slow — is a smaller/quantised open-vocab detector (or a WebGPU path) worth adding alongside for mobile?
Automated scan flags sub-24px tap targets on both classes — needs a manual tap-size verdict.

followUpGoals.goal: Capture a retained headless run of a real free-text query (e.g. 'a cat') with measured latency so real-inference can be evidenced at 5.
Evaluate a lighter open-vocabulary detector or a WebGPU fp16 path as an additive mobile-friendly companion demo.

summary: Retrospective code+conformance-grounded critique for owlv2-zero-shot-detection (Xenova/owlv2-base-patch16-ensemble, zero-shot-object-detection, q8, ~400 MB, Apache-2.0). Real open-vocabulary detection from a free-text query set, run off-thread; labelled canvas overlay, See-inside table, threshold slider filtering a cached run, shared auto-init loader, live status + on-page errors. Honest weaknesses: heavy ~400 MB download and slow WASM inference (surfaced in at-a-glance; performance kept off-thread), no fresh measured latency captured here, and sub-24px tap-target flags keeping support at needs-review.
